texts.full_text: performance. Narthaki Online interviews DSAiyyelu, master costumer for theIndian dance community the world over for nearly 50years. How did you get intodance tailoring? I joinedGemini Studios in1944and worked undercostumer Ambedkar.He taught me thebasics and I started working on costumes on my own from around 1946. Whatwas the first costumeyoudesigned for a dancer, or was it one of your colleagues? The last dance drama to be made in Gemini Studios' Dance Department – 'Aara VaazhVendum', which means "We must live" (written by poet Subramanya Bharathiyar) had me as its costumer. I usedcostumes fromthe studio for that film, but designed a new costume with goldand silverzariwork on black velvet cloth during my stintinGemini Studios itself – it was to be worn at an important scenewhere Goddess Kali wouldcome out of Siva's headwhen he does his tandavam (cosmic dance). Tell us about your earliest costumes for dancers. Whowere they designed? When did you start working with theworld renowned dancer Rukmini Devi Arundale,and how long have we worked together since then ? My first costume was ahalfsari that I madein 1946-7 (Idon't remember whichmonth) – it had brocade for blouse. The dance school where this girl studied belonged toDr RRajamaniAmmalwho is the sister of great musicianand singerSemmangudiSrinivasa Iyer,

texts.full_text: the control group, but the mortality rates were not reduced. This result led health policy groups to reconsider the role of sputum induction in early detection and screening strategies for lung cancer. While sputum induction demonstrated potential improvements in resectability and survival rates, the failure to reduce overall mortality highlighted the need for more refined diagnostic tools and interventions. The present invention addresses the limitations of traditional sputum induction methods by proposing an apparatus and method that enhances the quality and yield of sputum samples, leading to more accurate detection of early-stage lung cancer. The apparatus is designed to provide a controlled and consistent environment for inducing sputum, ensuring minimal patient discomfort while maximizing sample collection. texts.full_text: re the key enzymes which catalyze the last important step. Modification of ginsenoside aglycones by glycosyltransferases produces the complexity and diversity of ginsenosides, which have more extensive pharmacological activity. At present, ginsenoside aglycones and compound K have been obtained by synthetic biology. As the last step of ginsenoside biosynthesis, glycosylation of ginsenoside aglycones has been studied intensively in recent years.
